000_black_gram_anthracnose.jpg.label: Black Gram Anthracnose
000_black_gram_anthracnose.jpg.caption: Circular, black, sunken spots with dark center and bright red orange margins on leaves and pods. Severe infections spread entire leaf giving blighted appearance. Small, dark brown to black lesions on cotyledons; oval or eye-shaped lesions on stems which turn sunken and brown with purple to red margins.
000_black_gram_anthracnose.jpg.management: Plant resistant varieties; use certified disease-free seed; avoid sprinkler irrigation, water plants at base; plow bean crop debris into soil. Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Practice crop rotation. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation.

000_black_gram_leaf.jpg.label: Black Gram Leaf
000_black_gram_leaf.jpg.caption: Deep green, smooth leaves with even coloration. Leaves are firm and turgid. No spots, discoloration, or deformities. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_black_gram_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly but avoid waterlogging. Provide full sun (6-8 hours daily). Apply balanced fertilizer every 2-3 weeks. Maintain soil pH 6.0-6.8. Prune to improve air circulation. Monitor for early signs of pests or diseases.

001_black_gram_leaf.jpg.label: Black Gram Leaf
001_black_gram_leaf.jpg.caption: Deep green, smooth leaves with even coloration. Leaves are firm and turgid. No spots, discoloration, or deformities. Plant shows vigorous growth.

000_black_gram_leaf_crinckle.jpg.label: Black Gram Leaf Crinckle
000_black_gram_leaf_crinckle.jpg.caption: Leaves show crinkling and distortion. Leaf surfaces become irregular and wavy. Plants may show stunted growth.
000_black_gram_leaf_crinckle.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plants. Control aphid vectors that may transmit viruses. Use resistant varieties. Practice field sanitation. Avoid planting near infected crops.

001_black_gram_leaf_crinckle.jpg.label: Black Gram Leaf Crinckle
001_black_gram_leaf_crinckle.jpg.caption: Leaves show crinkling and distortion. Leaf surfaces become irregular and wavy. Plants may show stunted growth.

000_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.label: Black Gram Powdery Mildew
000_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.caption: White, powdery fungal growth on leaf surfaces, particularly on upper surfaces. Leaves may yellow and die prematurely. Severe infections can cover entire leaf surfaces.
000_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.management: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Apply sulfur-based fungicides or other appropriate fungicides. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Remove infected plant debris.

001_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.label: Black Gram Powdery Mildew
001_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.caption: White, powdery fungal growth on leaf surfaces, particularly on upper surfaces. Leaves may yellow and die prematurely. Severe infections can cover entire leaf surfaces.
001_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.management: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Apply sulfur-based fungicides or other appropriate fungicides. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Remove infected plant debris.

002_black_gram_powdery_mildew.jpg.label: Black Gram Powdery Mildew

000_black_gram_yellow_mosaic.jpg.label: Black Gram Yellow Mosaic
000_black_gram_yellow_mosaic.jpg.caption: Yellow mosaic patterns on leaves with distinct yellow patches or streaks. Leaves may show chlorosis. Plants become stunted and produce fewer pods.
000_black_gram_yellow_mosaic.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control whitefly vectors that transmit the virus. Use resistant varieties. Avoid planting near other infected crops. Practice crop rotation.

001_black_gram_yellow_mosaic.jpg.label: Black Gram Yellow Mosaic
001_black_gram_yellow_mosaic.jpg.caption: Yellow mosaic patterns on leaves with distinct yellow patches or streaks. Leaves may show chlorosis. Plants become stunted and produce fewer pods.

000_chili_cercospora.jpg.label: Chili Cercospora
000_chili_cercospora.jpg.caption: Small, circular spots on leaves with gray centers and brown margins. Spots may coalesce to form larger necrotic areas. Leaves may yellow and drop prematurely.
000_chili_cercospora.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_chili_mites_and_trips.jpg.label: Chili Mites And Trips
000_chili_mites_and_trips.jpg.caption: Silvery or bronze discoloration on leaves. Tiny white or yellow stippling marks. Curled or distorted leaves. Fine webbing may be visible (for mites). Black fecal spots on leaves.
000_chili_mites_and_trips.jpg.cause: Pests (Spider mites: Tetranychus spp.; Thrips: Thrips spp.)
000_chili_mites_and_trips.jpg.management: Apply neem oil or insecticidal soap. Use predatory mites for biological control. Maintain proper humidity levels. Remove heavily infested leaves. Apply appropriate miticides or insecticides if infestation is severe. Ensure good air circulation.

001_chili_mites_and_trips.jpg.label: Chili Mites And Trips
001_chili_mites_and_trips.jpg.caption: Silvery or bronze discoloration on leaves. Tiny white or yellow stippling marks. Curled or distorted leaves. Fine webbing may be visible (for mites). Black fecal spots on leaves.

000_chili_nutritional_deficiency.jpg.label: Chili Nutritional Deficiency
000_chili_nutritional_deficiency.jpg.caption: Yellowing of leaves (chlorosis), stunted growth, purple or reddish discoloration on leaves, leaf curling or distortion depending on specific nutrient lacking.
000_chili_nutritional_deficiency.jpg.cause: Nutritional deficiency (nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, magnesium, or micronutrients)
000_chili_nutritional_deficiency.jpg.management: Conduct soil testing to identify deficiency. Apply appropriate fertilizers or amendments. Ensure proper pH levels (6.0-6.8). Use balanced NPK fertilizers. Apply foliar sprays for quick correction.

001_chili_nutritional_deficiency.jpg.label: Chili Nutritional Deficiency

000_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.label: Chili Powdery Mildew
000_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.caption: White, powdery growth on underside of leaves which starts in patches but spreads across whole leaves, including top surface. Yellow-brown discoloration on underside of leaves. Leaves curling.
000_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.management: Apply appropriate fungicide if disease is severe. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Remove infected plant debris. Maintain proper humidity levels.

001_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.label: Chili Powdery Mildew
001_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.caption: White, powdery growth on underside of leaves which starts in patches but spreads across whole leaves, including top surface. Yellow-brown discoloration on underside of leaves. Leaves curling.
001_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.management: Apply appropriate fungicide if disease is severe. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Remove infected plant debris. Maintain proper humidity levels.

002_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.label: Chili Powdery Mildew
002_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.caption: White, powdery growth on underside of leaves which starts in patches but spreads across whole leaves, including top surface. Yellow-brown discoloration on underside of leaves. Leaves curling.
002_chili_powdery_mildew.jpg.management: Apply appropriate fungicide if disease is severe. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Remove infected plant debris. Maintain proper humidity levels.

000_guava_anthracnose.jpg.label: Guava Anthracnose
000_guava_anthracnose.jpg.caption: Dark, sunken lesions on fruit, leaves, and stems. Fruit lesions may have pink spore masses in center. Leaves show brown spots that may expand. Can cause fruit rot and defoliation.
000_guava_anthracnose.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected fruit and plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper pruning for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Harvest fruit carefully to prevent wounds.

001_guava_anthracnose.jpg.label: Guava Anthracnose
001_guava_anthracnose.jpg.caption: Dark, sunken lesions on fruit, leaves, and stems. Fruit lesions may have pink spore masses in center. Leaves show brown spots that may expand. Can cause fruit rot and defoliation.
001_guava_anthracnose.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected fruit and plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper pruning for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Harvest fruit carefully to prevent wounds.

002_guava_anthracnose.jpg.label: Guava Anthracnose
002_guava_anthracnose.jpg.caption: Dark, sunken lesions on fruit, leaves, and stems. Fruit lesions may have pink spore masses in center. Leaves show brown spots that may expand. Can cause fruit rot and defoliation.
002_guava_anthracnose.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected fruit and plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper pruning for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Harvest fruit carefully to prevent wounds.

000_guava_canker.jpg.label: Guava Canker
000_guava_canker.jpg.caption: Sunken, dark lesions on branches and stems. Bark becomes cracked and dies back. Lesions may girdle branches causing dieback. Gum may ooze from infected areas.
000_guava_canker.jpg.management: Prune and destroy infected branches well below canker area. Apply copper-based fungicides. Maintain tree vigor through proper fertilization and irrigation. Avoid mechanical damage to bark.

001_guava_canker.jpg.label: Guava Canker
001_guava_canker.jpg.caption: Sunken, dark lesions on branches and stems. Bark becomes cracked and dies back. Lesions may girdle branches causing dieback. Gum may ooze from infected areas.
001_guava_canker.jpg.management: Prune and destroy infected branches well below canker area. Apply copper-based fungicides. Maintain tree vigor through proper fertilization and irrigation. Avoid mechanical damage to bark.

002_guava_canker.jpg.label: Guava Canker

000_guava_dot.jpg.label: Guava Dot
000_guava_dot.jpg.caption: Small, dark, circular spots on fruit that may be slightly raised or sunken. Spots may coalesce to form larger lesions. Fruit quality is reduced.
000_guava_dot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected fruit. Apply appropriate fungicides. Ensure proper sanitation in orchard. Maintain tree health through proper care.

001_guava_dot.jpg.label: Guava Dot
001_guava_dot.jpg.caption: Small, dark, circular spots on fruit that may be slightly raised or sunken. Spots may coalesce to form larger lesions. Fruit quality is reduced.

000_guava_leaf.jpg.label: Guava Leaf
000_guava_leaf.jpg.caption: Glossy, dark green leaves with smooth margins. Leaves are firm and leathery. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_guava_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly during dry periods. Apply balanced fertilizer 3-4 times per year. Prune to maintain shape and remove dead wood. Mulch to conserve moisture. Monitor for pests and diseases.

001_guava_leaf.jpg.label: Guava Leaf
001_guava_leaf.jpg.caption: Glossy, dark green leaves with smooth margins. Leaves are firm and leathery. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.

000_guava_rust.jpg.label: Guava Rust
000_guava_rust.jpg.caption: Orange to brown pustules on leaves and fruit. Leaves may yellow and drop prematurely. Severe infections can defoliate trees and reduce fruit quality.
000_guava_rust.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based fungicides. Plant resistant varieties if available. Ensure proper spacing and air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation.

001_guava_rust.jpg.caption: Orange to brown pustules on leaves and fruit. Leaves may yellow and drop prematurely. Severe infections can defoliate trees and reduce fruit quality.

000_guava_scab.jpg.label: Guava Scab
000_guava_scab.jpg.caption: Dark, scabby lesions on fruit, leaves, and stems. Lesions may be raised and corky. Fruit becomes distorted and unmarketable. Leaves may show spots and drop.
000_guava_scab.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected fruit and plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper pruning for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain tree vigor.

001_guava_scab.jpg.label: Guava Scab

000_guava_styler_end_root.jpg.label: Guava Styler End Root
000_guava_styler_end_root.jpg.caption: Rotting at the stylar end (blossom end) of fruit. Fruit becomes soft and watery. Lesions may expand to cover large portions of fruit. Foul odor may be present.
000_guava_styler_end_root.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected fruit. Apply appropriate fungicides or bactericides. Ensure proper drainage. Avoid overhead irrigation. Harvest fruit at proper maturity.

000_malabar_spinach_anthracnose.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Anthracnose
000_malabar_spinach_anthracnose.jpg.caption: Dark, sunken lesions on leaves, stems, and petioles. Lesions may have concentric rings. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can cause plant death.
000_malabar_spinach_anthracnose.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_malabar_spinach_bacterial_spot.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Bacterial Spot
000_malabar_spinach_bacterial_spot.jpg.caption: Water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown and may have yellow halos. Spots may coalesce to form larger necrotic areas. Leaves may drop prematurely.
000_malabar_spinach_bacterial_spot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based bactericides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_malabar_spinach_downy_mildew.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Downy Mildew
000_malabar_spinach_downy_mildew.jpg.caption: Yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces with fuzzy, gray-purple growth on undersides. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
000_malabar_spinach_downy_mildew.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_malabar_spinach_leaf.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Leaf
000_malabar_spinach_leaf.jpg.caption: Large, succulent, heart-shaped leaves with smooth margins. Leaves are deep green and glossy. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_malabar_spinach_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly to keep soil moist. Provide partial to full sun. Apply balanced fertilizer every 2-3 weeks. Harvest leaves regularly to encourage new growth. Support vines with trellis.

000_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Pest Damage
000_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.caption: Chewed holes in leaves, skeletonized foliage, sticky residue on leaves, visible insects or webbing. Leaves may be distorted or discolored.
000_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.cause: Pests (caterpillars, aphids, mites, or other insects)
000_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.management: Apply neem oil or insecticidal soap. Handpick larger pests. Use row covers to prevent pest access. Encourage beneficial insects. Remove heavily damaged leaves.

000_okra_alternaria_leaf_spot.jpg.label: Okra Alternaria Leaf Spot
000_okra_alternaria_leaf_spot.jpg.caption: Dark brown to black circular spots on leaves with concentric rings. Spots may have yellow halos. Leaves may yellow and drop prematurely. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
000_okra_alternaria_leaf_spot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_okra_cercospora_leaf_spot.jpg.label: Okra Cercospora Leaf Spot
000_okra_cercospora_leaf_spot.jpg.caption: Small, circular spots with gray centers and brown margins on leaves. Spots may coalesce to form larger necrotic areas. Leaves may yellow and drop.
000_okra_cercospora_leaf_spot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_okra_downy_mildew.jpg.label: Okra Downy Mildew
000_okra_downy_mildew.jpg.caption: Yellow angular spots on upper leaf surfaces with fuzzy, gray-purple growth on undersides. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
000_okra_downy_mildew.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_okra_leaf.jpg.label: Okra Leaf
000_okra_leaf.jpg.caption: Large, palmate leaves with 5-7 lobes. Leaves are deep green and smooth. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_okra_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly but avoid waterlogging. Provide full sun (6-8 hours daily). Apply balanced fertilizer every 2-3 weeks. Maintain soil pH 6.0-6.8. Harvest pods when tender.

000_okra_leaf_curly_virus.jpg.label: Okra Leaf Curly Virus
000_okra_leaf_curly_virus.jpg.caption: Leaves become curled, twisted, and distorted. Plants may be stunted. Leaf margins may show yellowing. Reduced pod production.
000_okra_leaf_curly_virus.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control whitefly vectors that transmit the virus. Use resistant varieties if available. Avoid planting near infected crops.

000_onion_iris_yellow_virus.jpg.label: Onion Iris Yellow Virus
000_onion_iris_yellow_virus.jpg.caption: Yellow streaks on leaves, particularly at bases. Leaves may be flattened or crinkled. Bulbs are undersized. Flower stalks yellow and twisted.
000_onion_iris_yellow_virus.jpg.management: Control aphid vectors that transmit the virus. Plant virus-free sets or transplants. Remove infected plants. Plant more tolerant varieties. Practice field sanitation.

000_onion_leaf.jpg.label: Onion Leaf
000_onion_leaf.jpg.caption: Hollow, cylindrical leaves with uniform green coloration. Leaves are firm and upright. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_onion_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly but avoid waterlogging. Provide full sun. Apply balanced fertilizer. Maintain soil pH 6.0-7.0. Space plants properly for air circulation.

000_onion_leaf_blight.jpg.label: Onion Leaf Blight
000_onion_leaf_blight.jpg.caption: Water-soaked, dark green oval lesions or streaks on leaves. Tipburn of leaves. Dark spots on wrapper scales of bulbs. Reddish-brown discoloration of inner scales.
000_onion_leaf_blight.jpg.management: Avoid fertilizing plants during winter. Apply appropriate bacteriacidial sprays. Remove infected plants. Practice crop rotation. Ensure proper drainage.

000_onion_purple_blotch.jpg.label: Onion Purple Blotch
000_onion_purple_blotch.jpg.caption: Small water-soaked lesions on leaves or stalks with white centers. Lesions enlarge to become zonate and brown to purple with red or purple margin surrounded by yellow zone. Large lesions may coalesce and girdle leaf.
000_onion_purple_blotch.jpg.management: Avoid planting infected sets. Rotate crops to non-allium species for 3-4 years. Plant in well-draining areas. Destroy all infected crop debris. Apply appropriate foliar fungicides.

000_orka_phyllosticta_leaf_spot.jpg.label: Orka Phyllosticta Leaf Spot
000_orka_phyllosticta_leaf_spot.jpg.caption: Small, circular spots with dark borders and light centers on leaves. Spots may coalesce to form larger necrotic areas. Leaves may yellow and drop prematurely.
000_orka_phyllosticta_leaf_spot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_radish_black_leaf_spot.jpg.label: Radish Black Leaf Spot
000_radish_black_leaf_spot.jpg.caption: Dark brown or black circular spots on leaves with concentric rings. Spots may have yellow halos. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
000_radish_black_leaf_spot.jpg.management: Plant only certified, disease-free seed. Treat seeds with hot water prior to planting. Rotate crops to non-brassica species. Irrigate plants in morning to allow drying. Apply appropriate fungicide.

000_radish_downey_mildew.jpg.label: Radish Downey Mildew
000_radish_downey_mildew.jpg.caption: Yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces with fuzzy, white to gray growth on undersides. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
000_radish_downey_mildew.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

000_radish_flea_beetle.jpg.label: Radish Flea Beetle
000_radish_flea_beetle.jpg.caption: Small, shot-hole holes in leaves. Leaves may be skeletonized. Plants may be stunted. Severe infestations can destroy young seedlings.
000_radish_flea_beetle.jpg.management: Apply insecticidal sprays or dusts. Use row covers to protect young plants. Maintain field sanitation. Plant trap crops. Apply diatomaceous earth.

000_radish_fresh_leaf.jpg.label: Radish Fresh Leaf
000_radish_fresh_leaf.jpg.caption: Dark green, lobed leaves with smooth margins. Leaves are firm and turgid. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_radish_fresh_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly but avoid waterlogging. Provide full sun to partial shade. Apply balanced fertilizer. Maintain soil pH 6.0-7.0. Harvest leaves when young and tender.

000_radish_mosaic_virus.jpg.label: Radish Mosaic Virus
000_radish_mosaic_virus.jpg.caption: Mosaic patterns on leaves with light and dark green areas. Leaves may be distorted and stunted. Plants show reduced growth and poor root development.
000_radish_mosaic_virus.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plants. Control aphid vectors that transmit the virus. Use resistant varieties if available. Practice field sanitation. Avoid planting near infected crops.

000_wheat_leaf.jpg.label: Wheat Leaf
000_wheat_leaf.jpg.caption: Long, narrow leaves with parallel veins. Leaves are erect and green. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
000_wheat_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly during establishment. Apply nitrogen fertilizer as needed. Monitor for pests and diseases. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Harvest at proper maturity.

000_wheat_septoria.jpg.label: Wheat Septoria
000_wheat_septoria.jpg.caption: Brown to tan lesions on leaves with dark borders. Small black fruiting bodies (pycnidia) visible in lesions. Leaves may yellow and die prematurely. Severe infections can reduce yield.
000_wheat_septoria.jpg.management: Plant resistant varieties. Apply appropriate fungicides. Rotate crops with non-cereal species. Remove crop debris after harvest. Avoid overhead irrigation.

000_wheat_stripe_rust.jpg.label: Wheat Stripe Rust
000_wheat_stripe_rust.jpg.caption: Yellow to orange pustules arranged in stripes on leaves. Leaves may yellow and die prematurely. Severe infections can cause significant yield loss.
000_wheat_stripe_rust.jpg.management: Plant resistant varieties. Apply appropriate fungicides. Destroy alternate hosts. Plant early-maturing varieties. Monitor fields regularly for early detection.

001_malabar_spinach_anthracnose.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Anthracnose
001_malabar_spinach_anthracnose.jpg.caption: Dark, sunken lesions on leaves, stems, and petioles. Lesions may have concentric rings. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can cause plant death.
001_malabar_spinach_anthracnose.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

001_malabar_spinach_bacterial_spot.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Bacterial Spot
001_malabar_spinach_bacterial_spot.jpg.caption: Water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown and may have yellow halos. Spots may coalesce to form larger necrotic areas. Leaves may drop prematurely.
001_malabar_spinach_bacterial_spot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant material. Apply copper-based bactericides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

001_malabar_spinach_downy_mildew.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Downy Mildew
001_malabar_spinach_downy_mildew.jpg.caption: Yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces with fuzzy, gray-purple growth on undersides. Leaves may yellow and drop. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
001_malabar_spinach_downy_mildew.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

001_malabar_spinach_leaf.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Leaf
001_malabar_spinach_leaf.jpg.caption: Large, succulent, heart-shaped leaves with smooth margins. Leaves are deep green and glossy. No spots, yellowing, or distortion. Plant shows vigorous growth.
001_malabar_spinach_leaf.jpg.management: Water regularly to keep soil moist. Provide partial to full sun. Apply balanced fertilizer every 2-3 weeks. Harvest leaves regularly to encourage new growth. Support vines with trellis.

001_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.label: Malabar Spinach Pest Damage
001_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.caption: Chewed holes in leaves, skeletonized foliage, sticky residue on leaves, visible insects or webbing. Leaves may be distorted or discolored.
001_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.cause: Pests (caterpillars, aphids, mites, or other insects)
001_malabar_spinach_pest_damage.jpg.management: Apply neem oil or insecticidal soap. Handpick larger pests. Use row covers to prevent pest access. Encourage beneficial insects. Remove heavily damaged leaves.

001_okra_alternaria_leaf_spot.jpg.label: Okra Alternaria Leaf Spot
001_okra_alternaria_leaf_spot.jpg.caption: Dark brown to black circular spots on leaves with concentric rings. Spots may have yellow halos. Leaves may yellow and drop prematurely. Severe infections can defoliate plants.
001_okra_alternaria_leaf_spot.jpg.management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Practice crop rotation.
